title: rubus cuneatus
intro: Rubus cuneatus, also known as the Wedge-leaved Bramble, is a species of thorny shrub native to New Zealand. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to soothe and heal wounds, as well as reduce fever and alleviate digestive issues. The health benefits of Rubus cuneatus include its ability to act as an antioxidant, reduce oxidative stress, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of Rubus cuneatus typically involve infusing the leaves or berries in hot water to make a tea, or using the plant's roots to create a tincture or decoction.
